lesson: When calculating cash actually collected (money received in the bank), use the encaissement table.

When building telecom KPI queries, create a “top‑up tier” (or bracket) by bucketizing prepaid recharge amounts into monetary ranges and use this tiered column for metrics such as “Recharge by Tranche/Bracket/Top‑up by Tier”.
BSCS is the system that manages billing data.
Recharge *6 (also called Étoile 6 or Star 6) denotes the prepaid top‑up bundle that provides exclusive social‑media access.
Use the top‑up code “Recharge *3” to represent purchases of general mobile Internet data (4G/5G).
Recharge *3 is the top‑up code that denotes purchasing general mobile Internet data (4G/5G).
Recharge *3 is the top‑up code that denotes purchasing general mobile Internet data (4G/5G).
Recharge *2 is the specific top‑up code used for Voice Calls (Minutes).
When generating revenue for a specific recharge type over a period, always also calculate and include its percentage share of the total revenue for that same period.
For total overall recharge revenue calculations, always use the GLOBAL RECHARGE KPI.
